subject: Python Programming Language
description: Marking guidelines for Python coding exercises and assessments.
importantNote: Coding exercises are assessed on correctness, code quality, efficiency, and documentation.

general: Correctness: Does the code produce the expected output for all test cases?
Code Quality: Is the code readable, well-structured, and following PEP 8?
Efficiency: Is the algorithm reasonably efficient for the problem?
Documentation: Are there appropriate comments and docstrings?
Edge Cases: Does the code handle edge cases gracefully?

rules.condition: Code runs but produces incorrect output for some cases
rules.marksAwarded: Partial credit based on passing test cases

rules.condition: Missing error handling for edge cases

rules.marksAwarded: Deduct up to 10% for style issues

commonErrors: Shallow copy vs deep copy confusion

criteria.description: All operations correct, clear output formatting, variables properly named
All operations correct, but output formatting could be clearer
Most operations correct, 1-2 minor errors
Multiple operations incorrect, but basic structure present
Some attempt made, but largely incorrect
No attempt or completely incorrect
Perfect FizzBuzz implementation, correct order of conditions, clean code
Correct output but condition order could be improved, minor issues
Partially correct, some numbers misclassified
Basic loop structure present but logic flawed
No attempt or completely incorrect
Perfect implementation, handles punctuation correctly, case-insensitive, efficient
Correct frequency counts but minor issues (e.g., punctuation handling incomplete)
Basic counting works but misses some requirements
Significant issues, but some understanding shown
Minimal correct elements
No attempt or completely incorrect
Excellent OOP design, all features implemented correctly, clean inheritance
All features work, but some design could be improved
Most features work, but some missing or incorrect
Basic class structure present, but functionality limited
Attempt at classes made, but significant issues
Minimal OOP elements present
No attempt or completely incorrect
All generator implementations perfect, excellent use of itertools, insightful performance comparison
All features work correctly, minor improvements possible
Most features work, but some missing or incorrect
Basic generator implemented, but itertools usage limited
Significant issues, but demonstrates understanding of generators
Minimal correct elements
No attempt or completely incorrect
